articles.title: Read-only sandboxes that still trust the UI are security fanfiction

articles.title: Prompt injection is a flow problem, not a linguistic one
articles.summary:
The industry treats prompt injection as a linguistic failure. It approaches the problem as if the model simply needs better manners or more robust instruction following.

This is a category error.

If...

articles.title: Formal methods don't need better reward models. They need better checkers.
articles.summary:
I've been looking at how LLM efforts in formal methods struggle with semantic correctness. Most try to build better reward models to guess if a specification is right.

That is the wrong direction.

articles.title: Scaling models does not fix bad planning.
articles.summary:
A bigger model is not a better strategist. It is just a louder one.

The industry consensus is that if an agent fails, you give it more parameters,
